original_query: what is cost of sales
original_gold_answers: The accumulated total of all costs used to create a product or service, which has been sold.
query_pos: What is the cost of sales?
query_neg: What is the cost of sales excluding the cost of goods sold?

doc_gold.text: The cost of sales is the total cost used to create a product or service that has been sold, generally used by retailers. It does not include the cost of goods sold as defined by manufacturers.

doc_distractor.text: The cost of sales is the accumulated total of all costs used to create a product or service, which has been sold, and is essentially the same as the cost of goods sold for manufacturers.

answer_gold: The cost of sales excludes the cost of goods sold, focusing on retailer-related costs for sold products or services.

corpus.text: The muscular system is made up of tissues that work with the skeletal system to control movement of the body. original_query: parietal lobe location and primary function
original_gold_answers: The parietal lobe is located near the center of the brain, behind the frontal lobe, in front of the occipital lobe, and above the temporal lobe. The parietal lobe functions in processing sensory information from the various parts of the body. It is heavily related to the sense of touch and involved in the manipulation of objects, as well as in detecting the orientation and numbers of objects encountered.
query_pos: What is the location and primary function of the parietal lobe?
query_neg: What is the location and primary function of the parietal lobe excluding its role in language processing?

doc_gold.text: The parietal lobe is located near the center of the brain, behind the frontal lobe and in front of the occipital lobe. It primarily processes sensory information from the body, including touch, object manipulation, and orientation detection, without involving language functions.

doc_distractor.text: The parietal lobe is located at the back of the brain and is responsible for processing sensory information, interpreting visual information, and processing language and mathematics.

answer_gold: The parietal lobe is located near the center of the brain and its primary function is processing sensory information related to touch and object orientation, excluding language processing.

corpus.text: Cerebral atrophy refers to the progressive loss of brain cells over time. Atrophy refers to a decreased size or wasting away of any part of the body. Cerebral atrophy can happen in either the entire brain or in just one part of the brain and can lead to decreased brain mass and loss of neurological function. The symptoms of cerebral atrophy depend on the cause and location of cell death. Cerebral atrophy can occur due to brain injury, as in the case of stroke, or to a neurological disease, such as Alzheimer’s disease, cerebral palsy, or Huntington’s disease. Infections of the brain can also lead to the death of brain cells and cerebral atrophy

original_query: oven temperature and how long to bake a sweet potato
original_gold_answers: Heat oven to 400° F and bake a sweet potato for 45 minutes.
query_pos: What oven temperature and baking time is recommended to bake a sweet potato?

doc_gold.text: To avoid undercooking, do not bake sweet potatoes at temperatures below 350° F or for less than 30 minutes. The ideal baking is at 400° F for about 45 minutes to ensure tenderness.

doc_distractor.text: Heat oven to 400° F. Pierce each sweet potato several times and bake for about 45 minutes until tender. Make a slit in the top and top with butter and seasonings.

answer_gold: Do not bake sweet potatoes at temperatures below 350° F or for less than 30 minutes to avoid undercooking.
excluded_target: oven temperatures below 350° F and baking times less than 30 minutes

original_query: what is sacroiliitis symptoms
original_gold_answers: The most common symptoms of sacroiliitis include some combination of the following: 1 Fever. 2 Pain, usually low back pain, leg pain (may be in the front of the thigh), hip pain, and/or buttock pain.
query_pos: What are the common symptoms of sacroiliitis?
query_neg: What are the symptoms of sacroiliitis excluding fever?

doc_gold.text: Sacroiliitis symptoms commonly involve pain such as low back pain, leg pain in the front of the thigh, hip pain, and buttock pain, but do not include fever.

doc_distractor.text: The most common symptoms of sacroiliitis include fever and pain, typically low back pain, leg pain, hip pain, and buttock pain.

answer_gold: Pain such as low back pain, leg pain, hip pain, and buttock pain without fever.

original_query: how does a typical commercial lease work
original_gold_answers: The gross lease typically means a tenant pays one lump sum for rent, from which the landlord pays his expenses.The net lease has a smaller base rent, with other expenses paid for by the tenant.
query_pos: how does a typical commercial lease work
query_neg: how does a typical commercial lease work if it is not a gross lease

doc_gold.text: A net lease has a smaller base rent, with other expenses paid for by the tenant. Unlike a gross lease, the tenant is responsible for most of the expenses such as taxes, insurance, and maintenance.

doc_distractor.text: The gross lease typically means a tenant pays one lump sum for rent, from which the landlord pays his expenses including taxes, insurance, and maintenance.

answer_gold: A typical net lease requires the tenant to pay a smaller base rent plus other property expenses, unlike a gross lease where rent is all-inclusive.

corpus.text: Carpal tunnel syndrome (CTS) is a medical condition in which the median nerve is compressed as it travels through the wrist at the carpal tunnel and causes pain, numbness and tingling, in the part of the hand that receives sensation from the median nerve. The mechanism of injury is compression; there are a variety of contributing factors. Some of the individual predisposing factors include: diabetes, obesity, pregnancy, hypothyroidism, and a narrow-diameter carpal tunnel. CTS may also result from an injury that causes internal scarring or mis-aligned wrist bones.

corpus.text: Obsidian is a naturally occurring volcanic glass formed as an extrusive igneous rock. It is produced when felsic lava extruded from a volcano cools rapidly with minimum crystal growth. doc_gold.text: Applicants are not required to have a minimum credit score to qualify for an FHA loan; however, a lower score may affect loan terms.

answer_gold: There is no minimum credit score required to qualify for an FHA loan.
